prerequisites: A Ragie account. Sign up and log in to obtain access to the dashboard and API.
A Ragie API key. Create one from the API keys page at https://secure.ragie.ai/api-keys; it is supplied as the required `api_key` config field.

Source records to sync, with the column names you want Ragie to use for document content, document name, external id, and metadata (these map to the optional `content_fields`, `document_name_field`, `external_id_field`, `metadata_fields`, and `metadata_static` config fields).

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Ragie uses Bearer token authentication. The connector's required `api_key` secret field is sent as the `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>` header on every request to the Ragie API. Create the key on the Ragie API keys page and keep it secret.

setupSteps.title: Create a Ragie API key
setupSteps.body: Log in to Ragie and open the API keys page at https://secure.ragie.ai/api-keys. Generate a new API key and copy it. This value is used for the connector's required `api_key` field and is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connection endpoint
setupSteps.body: Paste the API key into the required `api_key` field. The connector targets the Ragie API at https://api.ragie.ai by default. Override the optional `api_url` field only if you need to point at a different Ragie API endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Map source fields to Ragie documents
setupSteps.body: Set the optional document-mapping fields to control how records become Ragie documents: `content_fields` for the source columns that make up the document body, `document_name_field` for the document name, `external_id_field` for a stable external identifier, and `metadata_fields` plus `metadata_static` for per-record and constant metadata.

setupSteps.title: Choose a partition and processing mode (optional)
setupSteps.body: Set the optional `partition` field to logically isolate synced documents in Ragie; retrievals scoped to that partition will only see these documents, and if omitted documents go to the `default` partition. Use the optional `processing_mode` field to select how Ragie processes uploaded documents.

sources.title: Ragie API Reference - Create Document (base URL https://api.ragie.ai)
Getting Started with Ragie's Python SDK for RAG (Ragie blog)
